instruments.prompt:
You are our in-house commercial counsel and we are the RECIPIENT. Review the attached agreement "Aegis-Mutual-NDA.docx" and redline it with tracked changes (apply_redline) to protect us. Read the whole agreement first, then work through every clause that is one-sided against us — in particular:
  - make the obligations genuinely mutual (it is one-directional despite the title)
  - add the standard exclusions (public domain, already known, independently developed, required by law)
  - cap the perpetual confidentiality term to a fixed period
  - allow retention of backup and legally-required copies on return
  - make the injunctive-relief and no-bond right reciprocal
  - narrow or mutualise the one-sided indemnity
Make balanced, defensible edits a partner would accept. Apply ALL your edits in a SINGLE apply_redline call (it re-redlines the original on each call, so one call must cover everything).

You are our in-house commercial counsel and we are the LICENSEE. Review the attached agreement "DataBridge-Licence.docx" and redline it with tracked changes (apply_redline) to protect us. Read the whole agreement first, then work through every clause that is one-sided against us — in particular:
  - the suspension-at-sole-discretion-without-notice right
  - the unilateral fee increase and non-refundable fees
  - the IP vesting of configurations and feedback in the Licensor
  - the perpetual Licensee Content licence (especially model training)
  - the "as is" warranty disclaimer
  - the one-sided indemnity (make it reciprocal; add Licensor IP cover)
  - the three-month liability cap (carve out confidentiality, IP and data)
  - the auto-renewal and no-termination-for-convenience term
Make balanced, defensible edits a partner would accept. Apply ALL your edits in a SINGLE apply_redline call (it re-redlines the original on each call, so one call must cover everything).

You are our in-house commercial counsel and we are the CONTROLLER. Review the attached agreement "Northwind-DPA.docx" and redline it with tracked changes (apply_redline) to protect us. Read the whole agreement first, then work through every clause that is one-sided against us — in particular:
  - require processing only on the Controller's documented instructions
  - strike the Processor's own-purpose use and model-training rights
  - require prior authorisation and flow-down for sub-processors, with the Processor remaining liable
  - require appropriate technical and organisational measures to the risk
  - shorten breach notification to without undue delay and add assistance
  - add a Controller audit right
  - require transfer safeguards (e.g. SCCs) for international transfers
  - require deletion or return on termination with no open-ended retention
Make balanced, defensible edits a partner would accept. Apply ALL your edits in a SINGLE apply_redline call (it re-redlines the original on each call, so one call must cover everything).

You are our in-house commercial counsel and we are the CUSTOMER. Review the attached agreement "SecureScan-MSA.docx" and redline it with tracked changes (apply_redline) to protect us. Read the whole agreement first, then work through every clause that is one-sided against us — in particular:
  - the unilateral fee increase and non-refundable fees
  - the auto-renewal and no-termination-for-convenience term
  - the IP assignment of deliverables and feedback to the Vendor
  - the perpetual Customer Data licence
  - the "as is" warranty disclaimer
  - the one-sided indemnity (make it reciprocal; add Vendor IP cover)
  - the one-month liability cap (carve out confidentiality, IP and gross negligence; raise the period)
Make balanced, defensible edits a partner would accept. Apply ALL your edits in a SINGLE apply_redline call (it re-redlines the original on each call, so one call must cover everything).
